notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for switzerland. This file is the source of truth for this country's visitor/tourism visa knowledge and is designed to evolve alongside the country-specific form-filling workflow.

documents.title: Switzerland Schengen Visa for a Stay of up to 90 Days

chunks.title: Switzerland short-stay Schengen route
chunks.content: The Swiss FDFA China page has a dedicated Schengen visa route for a stay of up to 90 days and links to nationality-based visa provisions, where to apply, application procedure, documentation, fees, and data information. For a China-resident applicant going to Switzerland for short tourism, VIZA AI should use the Switzerland Schengen short-stay route and tell the user to confirm requirements by nationality/residence and submit through the competent authority or visa application centre.

chunks.title: Switzerland application channel in China
chunks.content: The Swiss FDFA China page points users to VFS Global for visa services and lists Swiss representations/consular districts in China. Where to apply can depend on place of residence, so VIZA AI should ask where the applicant legally resides before giving appointment instructions.

chunks.title: 中国护照瑞士短期旅游签证建议
chunks.content: 中国护照持有人如果去瑞士短期旅游，且瑞士是唯一目的地或主要停留国家，通常应按 Switzerland/Schengen 90 天以内短期签证路线准备，而不是美国、英国或越南签证。VIZA AI 应先确认国籍、居住地、瑞士是否为主目的地、停留天数、是否同时去法国/意大利等其他申根国家；常见材料方向包括护照、申请表、照片、旅行医疗保险、行程/住宿、资金证明、回国约束力、指纹采集，并按居住地选择对应瑞士签证申请渠道。

chunks.title: Where and when to apply for Switzerland/Schengen
chunks.content: A Schengen visa is for a short temporary visit of up to 90 days in any 180-day period. The EU guidance says to apply at the consulate of the country intended to visit; when visiting multiple Schengen countries, apply to the country where the traveller will spend the longest time, or the first-entry country if stays are equal. Applications should be submitted at least 15 days before travel and no earlier than 6 months before the intended journey.

chunks.title: Switzerland/Schengen documents, fees, and processing
chunks.content: Common Schengen application documents include a passport valid at least 3 months after planned departure from the Schengen area, a visa application form, ICAO-compliant photo, travel medical insurance covering emergency medical care/hospitalisation/repatriation, purpose-of-stay documents, evidence of financial means and accommodation, evidence of intention to return, and fingerprints. EU guidance lists the adult visa fee as EUR 90 and normal processing as 15 days, extendable up to 45 days if more examination or documents are needed.

documents.title: Switzerland Application Form and Document Intake Requirements

chunks.title: Switzerland application channel and form scope
chunks.content: Use the competent Switzerland consulate or its authorised visa application centre when Switzerland is the only destination, main destination, longest-stay destination, or first-entry state when stays are equal. Also check the country-specific source: https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/china/en/home/visa/entry-ch/up-90-days.html. Official source URLs reviewed for this seed: https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/5fe79baa-2d46-4b65-84ec-ea2e9b5ab1d6_en?filename=Visa+Application+Form.pdf ; https://www.eda.admin.ch/countries/china/en/home/visa/entry-ch/up-90-days.html ; https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/policies/schengen/visa-policy/applying-schengen-visa_en. This RAG chunk is for visitor/tourism-oriented intake and form-filling preparation; route work, study, residence, immigration, journalism or other regulated purposes to a separate country workflow.

chunks.title: Switzerland fields to collect before filling the form
chunks.content: The harmonised Schengen form intake should collect surname/current and birth names, first names, date/place/country of birth, current and birth nationality, sex, civil status, parental authority for minors, national ID where applicable, travel document type/number/issue/expiry/issuing authority, home address/email/phone, residence in another country if any, occupation, employer or school, travel purpose, destination Member State(s), first-entry Member State, number of entries requested, intended arrival/departure dates and duration, prior Schengen fingerprints, entry permit for final destination if any, inviting person/company or hotel/accommodation, who pays travel/living costs, EU/EEA/Swiss family member data if applicable, and applicant declaration/signature.

chunks.title: Switzerland supporting documents and review checklist
chunks.content: Core Schengen evidence includes signed visa application form, passport/travel document valid beyond the planned Schengen exit, photo, travel medical insurance, proof of purpose, itinerary/transport, accommodation or host invitation, evidence of financial means, evidence of intention to return, residence/jurisdiction proof, fee, and biometrics where required. Workflow guardrails: VIZA should determine the competent Schengen state before filling forms. For multi-country trips, compute longest stay or first entry if equal; collect day split and accommodation per country. Do not submit or sign declarations without explicit user confirmation.
